hints.Varium: 'Varium' is a predicate adjective (neuter) — it describes the subject
hints.mutabile: 'mutabile' is a predicate adjective — it describes the subject

hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'femina' would be 'femina' (same form), but it's the subject
hints.Vocative: Vocative of 'femina' would be 'femina' (same form), but this is a general statement

hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'femina' with -a ending is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — 'femina' is a typical 1st declension -a noun
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'femina' is 1st declension

hints.Masculine: Masculine nominative would be 'varius' — the -um ending is neuter
hints.Feminine: Feminine nominative would be 'varia' — the -um ending is neuter
hints.Common: 'Varium' has a specific neuter ending — it's used as a neuter predicate with abstract force

hints.Accusative: 'mutabile' neuter nominative and accusative are identical, but it's a predicate adjective here
hints.Vocative: This is a general statement, not an address

hints.Adjective: Adjectives modify nouns and decline — 'semper' is indeclinable
hints.Conjunction: Conjunctions join clauses — 'semper' modifies the implied verb
hints.Preposition: Prepositions govern nouns — 'semper' doesn't take an object
Prepositions govern nouns in a specific case — 'et' joins words or clauses
hints.Adverb: 'et' can sometimes mean 'also' (adverbial), but here it joins 'Varium' and 'mutabile'
hints.Interjection: Interjections are exclamations — 'et' is a coordinating conjunction

translation: Woman is ever fickle and changeable
